root.description: Single persistent channel. On connect the daemon pushes Hello; DaemonEvents follow as they happen. The client may send ClientMessages at any time.

AgentEvent.description: An event emitted by [`Agent::run`]. Owned, so a handler can store or serialize it past the `emit` call.

oneOf.description: Incremental assistant text.
Incremental assistant reasoning ("thinking").
Reasoning finished: the model stopped thinking and began answering (or calling tools). Carries the wall-clock interval it thought, so a frontend can show "thought for Ns" the moment thinking ends.
The assistant turn, assembled and appended to the conversation.
A tool call about to run (after approval, if any).
A finished (or denied) tool call, with its output fed back.

message.description: Exact tool-result message recorded in the conversation.

oneOf.description: Final answer reached (no more tool calls).

rounds.description: Rounds taken to reach the answer.

ClientInfo.description:
Identifies the frontend that created a session: its product name and version.

Every client sends this on [`ClientMessage::CreateSession`]; it is persisted with the session and rides on [`SessionInfo::created_by`], so any client (and a restart) can tell which frontend opened a given session.

version.description: Frontend version string, e.g. its crate or package version.

ClientMessage.description: A message a client sends to the daemon.

oneOf.description: Opens (or finds) the workspace rooted at `path`; the daemon canonicalizes the path and broadcasts a [`DaemonEvent::WorkspaceCreated`].

path.description: Filesystem path of the workspace root.

oneOf.description: Creates a session whose cwd is the workspace at `path`, opening that workspace first if it is new; broadcasts a [`DaemonEvent::SessionCreated`].

client.description: The frontend creating the session, recorded as its creator.

config.description: What to build the session from.

path.description: Filesystem path of the workspace the session runs in.

oneOf.description: Requests one session's full message backlog; answered by [`ServerMessage::History`].
Routes an [`EngineCommand`] to a session's engine.

command.description: The command to deliver to that session's engine.

oneOf.description: Fetches one media blob's bytes by content hash, for a client rendering media a message references; answered by [`ServerMessage::Blob`].

hash.description: Content hash of the blob to fetch (as carried on a message's media ref).

oneOf.description: Re-scans the daemon's profiles and answers with [`ServerMessage::Profiles`]. The `Hello` snapshot already carries the list at connect time; this refreshes it without reconnecting, so a profile added on disk after connect shows up.
Deletes a session; broadcasts a [`DaemonEvent::SessionRemoved`].
Deletes a workspace and every session under it; broadcasts a [`DaemonEvent::SessionRemoved`] per session, then [`DaemonEvent::WorkspaceRemoved`].

ClientMessage.title: ClientMessage

ContentPart.description: A single part of a multipart message.

oneOf.description: A run of UTF-8 text.
An image to show the model.

source.description: Where the image bytes come from.

oneOf.description: An audio clip for audio-input models.

format.description: Audio container format, such as `wav` or `mp3`.

source.description: Where the audio bytes come from.

oneOf.description: A file (e.g. a PDF) to attach.

filename.description: Optional original filename, shown to the provider when supported.

source.description: Where the file bytes come from.

DaemonEvent.description: A lifecycle or content event on the daemon's single broadcast stream, sent to every connected client so all frontends share one live view.

oneOf.description: A workspace was opened.
A workspace was closed and removed.
A session was created.
A session was removed.
A session's summary changed (title, message count, model, or permission mode).
A content event from one session's engine (token deltas, tool calls).

EngineCommand.description: A command a frontend sends to the engine.

oneOf.description: A new user message that should drive an assistant turn. `model`, when set, names a catalog entry the session switches to before the turn (and stays on for later turns); `None` keeps the current model.

content.description: The user's content: plain text or multimodal parts.

model.description: Catalog name to switch to for this and subsequent turns, if any.

reasoning.description: Reasoning level to switch to for this and subsequent turns, if any. Must be one the active model offers (after any `model` switch above), else the turn fails with [`ErrorCode::UnsupportedReasoning`].

oneOf.description: A response to a pending tool-approval request.

allow.description: Whether the call is allowed to run.

id.description: Id of the tool call being decided.

remember.description: Whether to remember this answer as an "allow always" rule.

oneOf.description: Changes the session's permission mode, effective immediately: a turn already running picks it up at its next tool-call gate.

mode.description: The mode the session switches to.

oneOf.description: Cancels the in-flight turn.

Evaluates `code` against the session's scripting VM, answered by an [`EngineEvent::EvalResult`] tagged with the same `id`. Runs serialized with turns: an eval sent mid-turn waits until the turn ends, and while an eval runs nothing else on the session is processed.

id.description: Client-chosen correlation id, echoed on the result.

EngineEvent.description: An event the engine streams back to a frontend: an agent-loop event passed through, or an engine-level signal the agent never produces.

oneOf.description: The user message that started a turn, echoed so every client and the transcript record it through the same path as assistant and tool events.
An event from the agent loop, forwarded unchanged.
A tool call awaiting the frontend's approval.
The session switched to a different catalog model (its new name).
The session switched to a different reasoning level (its new value).
The session's permission mode changed (its new value).
A user message accepted while a turn was in flight, held to run after it.
The in-flight turn was canceled by the frontend, with the wall-clock interval it ran before the cancel landed.

timing.description: Interval the turn ran before cancellation.

oneOf.description: The turn failed. `code` is the machine-readable category a frontend branches on; `message` is the human-readable description. The `[started_at_ms, ended_at_ms]` interval is how long it ran before failing.

timing.description: Interval the turn ran before failure.

oneOf.description: The result of an [`EngineCommand::Eval`], tagged with its correlation id.

ErrorCode.description: A machine-readable category for a turn failure, so a frontend can react (retry, reauthenticate, reopen the model picker) without parsing the message.

oneOf.description: The turn was canceled by the frontend.
The provider rate-limited the request; back off and retry.
The request timed out; retry.
A network/connectivity failure; retry.
Credentials were missing, invalid, or rejected; fix the API key.
The selected model is not in the session's catalog; pick another.
The requested reasoning level is not one the active model supports.
The model kept calling tools past the round cap.
The provider rejected or failed the request.
The endpoint's protocol or response stream could not be handled.
An internal failure (I/O, serialization, or an unclassified error).

EvalOutcome.description:
The result of evaluating a snippet against a session's scripting VM.

`Ok` carries the evaluated value (serialized to JSON, falling back to a string representation for values JSON cannot hold) plus anything the snippet printed; `Err` carries the failure message (a load error, a runtime error, or that eval is not enabled for the session).

oneOf.description: The snippet ran to completion.

value.description: The returned value as JSON, or a string repr for unserializable values.

oneOf.description: The snippet failed (or eval is disabled).

MediaSource.description: Where a media part's bytes come from.

oneOf.description: A remote `http(s)` URL or an inline `data:` URL, kept verbatim.

url.description: The URL, passed to the provider as-is.

oneOf.description: Inline base64-encoded bytes with their MIME type.

mime.description: MIME type of the data, such as `image/png`.

oneOf.description: A content-addressed reference into the blob store, resolved to inline bytes at send time so the transcript and firehose carry only the ref.

bytes.description: Size of the referenced bytes, for display without loading them.

hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the bytes, naming the blob.

mime.description: MIME type of the referenced bytes.

Message.description: One chat message sent to or received from a model.

content.description: Content for this message: plain text or multimodal parts.

meta.description: Provenance for a received assistant turn; `None` for turns you authored.

reasoning_content.description: Reasoning content; empty when absent. Saved to JSONL, never re-sent.

tool_call_id.description: For a `tool` message, the id of the tool call it answers.

tool_calls.description: Tool calls requested by an assistant turn.

Meta.description: Provenance and timing for a received turn. Saved to JSONL, never re-sent.

first_token_at_ms.description: Epoch ms of the first streamed token (text or reasoning) on an assistant turn. Time-to-first-token is measured from `timing.started_at_ms`.

reasoning_timing.description: Reasoning interval on an assistant turn.

ModelInfo.description: One selectable model the daemon advertises, for a client's model picker. Names a catalog entry; carries no endpoint or credentials.

default_reasoning.description: The level a new session starts on for this model (one of `reasoning_levels`), or `None` when the model has no reasoning knob.

name.description: Catalog name the client selects this model by.

protocol.description: Wire protocol the model's endpoint speaks (for display only).

reasoning_levels.description: Reasoning levels this model accepts, lowest to highest, for a client's picker. Empty when the model has no reasoning knob. The set varies by backend (e.g. the Codex backend and the API offer different levels).

PermissionMode.description: How aggressively a session auto-approves tool calls.

oneOf.description: Approve everything except a hard deny.
Approve unless Lua asks or denies. The default.
Ask for anything not explicitly allowed.

Role.description: Role of a message in a chat-style conversation.

oneOf.description: System-level instruction.
User turn.
Assistant turn.
Tool result fed back to the model.

ServerMessage.description: A message the daemon sends to a client.

oneOf.description: Sent once on connect: a snapshot of every workspace and session, so a fresh client can render the full tree before the first [`DaemonEvent`].

models.description: Every model the client may select, from the daemon's catalog.

profiles.description: Profiles the client may pick when creating a session, by name. Always includes `"default"` (the daemon's `init.lua`), then each `init_<name>.lua`.

oneOf.description: Direct reply to [`ClientMessage::CreateSession`], telling the requesting client the id of the session it just created. The broadcast [`DaemonEvent::SessionCreated`] reaches every client and so cannot be attributed to one creator; this ack goes only to the caller.

session.description: Summary of the newly created session.

oneOf.description: One session's full conversation, answering [`ClientMessage::History`].
A broadcast event from the daemon's firehose.
One media blob's bytes, answering [`ClientMessage::Blob`]. The MIME type travels on the message's media reference, so only the bytes are returned.

hash.description: Content hash the bytes were requested by.

oneOf.description: The current profile list, answering [`ClientMessage::ListProfiles`]. Same shape as `Hello`'s `profiles`, sent only to the requesting client.
A transport-level failure (unknown id, malformed frame, bad path), distinct from [`EngineEvent::Error`], a turn failure already in the stream.

session.description: Session the fault relates to, if any.

ServerMessage.title: ServerMessage

SessionConfig.description:
What a client may choose when creating a session.

Carries no credentials: the daemon resolves the endpoint, auth, and tool set from its own trusted config (`~/.yuke/init.lua`), so a remote client never ships a key or Lua. The optional fields override the config's defaults.

max_rounds.description: Caps tool-calling rounds per turn; `None` means unlimited.

permission.description: Permission mode for this session; `None` takes the daemon default.

profile.description: Which profile supplies the base Lua config: `"<name>"` loads `~/.yuke/init_<name>.lua`; `None` (or `"default"`) loads `~/.yuke/init.lua`. Persisted so a rehydrated session rebuilds on the same profile. If a named profile is later removed, rehydration permanently migrates the session to the default profile. The workspace's `.yuke/init.lua` still overlays on top.

reasoning.description: Forces the session's initial reasoning level. Applied where the chosen model offers it (clamped to the model's default otherwise), then persisted so a rehydrated session restores the same level. `None` takes the model's default.

SessionInfo.description: A one-line summary of a session, for a client's session picker.

created_by.description: The frontend that created this session, recorded at create time and kept for the session's life.

message_count.description: Number of messages accumulated so far.

model.description: Model the session drives turns with.

permission.description: Permission mode the session is currently running in.

reasoning.description: Reasoning level the session currently drives turns with; `""` when the model has no reasoning knob. Cross-reference [`ModelInfo::reasoning_levels`] for the choices available on the current model.

title.description: Display title, derived from the first user message.

oneOf.description: Natural end.
Hit the output token limit.
Stopped by a content filter.
Stopped to make tool calls.
Unknown or unrecognized stop reason.

TimeSpan.description: A completed wall-clock interval in Unix epoch milliseconds.

ended_at_ms.description: Epoch ms when the operation ended.

started_at_ms.description: Epoch ms when the operation began.

ToolCall.description: One tool call requested by the model.

arguments.description: Tool arguments as a raw JSON string, parsed by the caller.

id.description: Provider-assigned id, echoed back on the matching tool result.

name.description: Name of the tool to invoke.

Usage.description: Token usage reported by a provider.

WorkspaceInfo.description: A one-line summary of a workspace, for a client's workspace picker.

root.description: Canonical absolute path of the workspace root (each session's cwd).

title.description: Display title, defaulting to the root directory's name.

info.description: WebSocket protocol between the yuke daemon and its clients. One JSON ClientMessage or ServerMessage per WebSocket text frame.
info.title: yuke daemon
